phases.result:
Planning pipeline complete. The Story is persisted and `agent::ready`.

## Summary

**Story #3** — *Add POST /password so signed-in users can change their password* — created and marked `agent::ready`.
[github.com/.../issues/3](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-23-story-scope-mandrel-dd97c6f3/issues/3)

**Pipeline (planning only, no delivery):**
- **Interrogate** → route `lite` (trivial single-artifact scope), no duplicate Stories.
- **Author** → one Story (default-single policy; the work is cohesive, not independently sliceable).
- **Critics** → consolidation & pre-mortem skipped (ledgered — 1 story, no risk-heuristic/external-dep triggers); text-hygiene clean.
- **Persist** → dry-run caught a `verify[]` tier-suffix violation (fixed), then all deterministic gates passed and the live persist created the issue.

**What the Story specifies** (grounded in the actual code in `src/server.js` / `src/store.js`):
- `POST /password`, authed via `Authorization: Bearer <token>` → `store.userForSession` like the other protected routes; missing/unknown token → 401 (before any body handling).
- Body `{ "newPassword": <non-empty string> }` parsed via existing `readJsonBody` → wrong Content-Type / empty / oversized / malformed all 400; non-string/empty `newPassword` → 400.
- Success → 200, no password echoed.
- **Core security invariant:** a new store method (`changePassword`) rotates the credential in one operation — re-hash with `hashPassword` (scrypt + per-user salt, never plaintext) **and** delete all of the user's session rows including the authenticating token. So pre-change sessions → 401, login with the new password works, login with the old password → 401.
- Non-goals recorded (no current-password re-auth, no strength policy, no reset flow, no rate-limit/audit).

**One warning worth noting:** the `route::lite` label couldn't be attached (`gh` label-ensure failed with exit 1) — the issue was still created without it. Add the label by hand if you want the marker; it doesn't affect delivery.

Deliver later (not this session) with `/deliver 3`.

Delivered. 

## `/deliver 3 --yes` — complete

The planning pipeline had produced a single v2 Story (the ticket model is Story-only — no separate Epic), so delivery resolved to **Story #3** and ran it through the one delivery engine.

**Outcome — `landed`:**
- **PR #4** → merged into `main` (squash + required checks green)
- Story #3 → **`agent::done`**, issue closed
- Gates: validation ✅ · base-sync ✅ · code-review ✅
- Branch `story-3` → PR → `main` (the only sanctioned merge surface)

**What shipped** (per the existing plan, not re-authored): `POST /password` for signed-in users — bearer-auth'd like the other endpoints, JSON `{"newPassword": ...}` via `readJsonBody`, a `changePassword(userId, newPassword)` store method that rotates the scrypt hash (per-user salt, no plaintext) and deletes all of the user's sessions in one operation. Post-change: pre-change tokens 401, login with the new password succeeds, old password 401; bad requests stay 400 with the server up. AC-1..AC-6 verified via the acceptance self-eval and the full close chain.

One non-blocking note from the terminal envelope: the post-land `main` fast-forward was skipped because the working tree is dirty (the untracked `temp/` scratch dir) — cosmetic only; the merge itself is complete on the remote. Single-Story run, so no per-run epilogue applies.
